spec.reasoning: The task depends on blank row positions as a risk flag, so we must NOT insert blank rows (which would alter the blank-row-separated ranges the macro must detect). Instead we use two safe perturbation types: (1) Lexical rename — rename columns 'Number'->'Code' and 'Name'->'Person', rename sheet 'Sheet1'->'DataSheet'. These are same-level descriptiveness (single full words). The header names 'Number' and 'Name' appear as data values at rows 16, 43, and 58 (sub-headers within the data), so those must also be renamed. The word 'names' appears in the instruction as general English but 'Name' as a column header is a direct reference, and 'Number' likewise — both are safe to rename since the instruction references them as 'Column A' and 'Column B' positionally, not by header name. (2) Distractor column — add an empty 'Category' column after the last used column. This tests whether the agent hardcodes column count or correctly identifies only columns A and B (now with renamed headers) as the data columns. No blank rows are inserted or removed, preserving the task's core logic of sorting non-blank ranges separated by blank rows.
